evals.expected_output: stdout containing 1099511627776, exit code 0, no stderr.

evals.prompt: Fetch https://example.com/data.csv, compute the mean of column 'price', and return the result.
evals.expected_output: Container fetches the URL, installs pandas, reads the CSV, computes the mean, and returns it. Output also includes a download URL for any saved artifact.
assertions: Dependencies (pandas, requests or similar) are installed via pip in the container
The Python snippet is written to a file via container_file_write rather than passed inline if longer than a one-liner
The mean is reported in stdout or read back from a JSON output file
If an output file is produced, container_file_download_url is called and the URL is returned

evals.prompt: Run a script that takes 4 minutes — make sure it doesn't time out.
evals.expected_output: Long-running script is forked into the background, status is polled via container_ping or file reads, and the user is told the script is still running rather than the call hanging.
assertions: The script is written to a file via container_file_write, not passed inline
The script is launched in the background (e.g. via nohup or & redirection), not blocked on inline
The response explains the polling strategy rather than blocking on a single long container_exec call
